If you want to restrict overall_rating to an expected scale (e.g., 1-5), specify that and we will add a filter.","submitted_at timezone is used as stored; no additional timezone normalization is applied.","If you prefer a different academic-year boundary (e.g., Sept 1 start) or a calendar-year grouping, specify and the SQL expression for academic_year will be adjusted."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"WITH ev AS (
  SELECT
    submitted_at,
    overall_rating,
    CASE
      WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM submitted_at) >= 8
      THEN (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int)::text || '-' || (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int + 1)::text
      ELSE ((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int - 1)::text || '-' || (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int)::text)
    END AS academic_year,
    CASE
      WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM submitted_at) >= 8
      THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int
      ELSE (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int - 1)
    END AS start_year
  FROM public.course_evaluations
  WHERE overall_rating IS NOT NULL
    AND submitted_at IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  academic_year AS academic_year,
  ROUND(AVG(overall_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_overall_rating
FROM ev
GROUP BY academic_year, start_year
ORDER BY start_year ASC;","validationIssues":["X encoding uses type "ordinal" for academic_year. visualization.title: Average Overall Evaluation Rating by Academic Year

resultRaw.sql:
WITH ev AS (
  SELECT
    submitted_at,
    overall_rating,
    CASE
      WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM submitted_at) >= 8
      THEN (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int)::text || '-' || (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int + 1)::text
      ELSE ((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int - 1)::text || '-' || (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int)::text)
    END AS academic_year,
    CASE
      WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM submitted_at) >= 8
      THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int
      ELSE (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM submitted_at)::int - 1)
    END AS start_year
  FROM public.course_evaluations
  WHERE overall_rating IS NOT NULL
    AND submitted_at IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  academic_year AS academic_year,
  ROUND(AVG(overall_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_overall_rating
FROM ev
GROUP BY academic_year, start_year
ORDER BY start_year ASC;

The SQL orders results by start_year, but start_year is not included in the final output schema; if you rely on explicit sort preservation in the chart, include start_year (or add an explicit sort) to guarantee chronological ordering in all environments.
